sprint: Pattern Challenge Polish -- No Scrollbars, Circular Glowing Tap Targets
task: A direct, same-session follow-up: 'no scroll bars please. and the gid can be in circle also a lighten up circle.' Measured directly that the Identity Gate's inline pattern-tap challenge genuinely overflowed and scrolled on shorter viewport heights, since its tap board was sized by width only, never by the viewport's own height. Fixed by capping the board's size with a viewport-height-relative min() (matching Creation Flow's own established no-scroll-screens technique) so it shrinks on a short screen instead of forcing a scrollbar -- verified zero overflow down to 520-560px viewport height for every realistic state of the challenge, with overflow-y:auto left only as a disclosed last-resort safety net. Also redesigned the tap targets from flat rounded squares into true circles that genuinely 'light up' when tapped -- a radial-gradient glowing orb with a soft box-shadow bloom and a brief grow-in animation on selection, plus a subtle hover brighten, both skipped under prefers-reduced-motion. Verified via the full existing pattern-challenge and Magic Card Playwright suites plus a real screenshot. Confined to css/style.css.
